description: Test cases for subterm hoisting and case statement hoisting

literal.string: Test cases for subterm hoisting and case statement hoisting
hoistNothing: let with list in body unchanged
hoistNothing: let with application in body unchanged
hoistLists: list in body is hoisted into local let
hoistLists: multiple lists in body are hoisted together
hoistLists: list in binding value is hoisted into local let
hoistLists: nested lists hoisted from inside out
hoistApplications: application in list element is hoisted
hoistApplications: application in record field is hoisted
hoistApplications: nested applications hoisted from inside out
hoistCaseStatements: case in application argument is hoisted
hoistCaseStatements: case in list element is hoisted
hoistLists: nested let - inner let processed independently
hoistLists: term referring to let-bound variable needs no capture
hoistLists: term referring to lambda above let needs no capture
hoistLists: lambda-bound var not free in hoisted term needs no capture
hoistLists: lambda-bound var free in hoisted term requires capture
hoistLists: only free lambda-bound vars are captured
hoistLists: stable naming for binding and body
hoistLists: stable naming for multiple bindings
hoistLists: polymorphic binding with self-reference below hoisted term
case at top level of let body is NOT hoisted
case in let binding value is NOT hoisted
case inside lambda body is NOT hoisted
case inside nested lambdas is NOT hoisted
case as LHS of one application is NOT hoisted
case wrapped in annotation is NOT hoisted
case in lambda with one application is NOT hoisted
case as RHS of application IS hoisted
case in nested application LHS IS hoisted
case inside list element IS hoisted
case inside lambda inside list IS hoisted
list inside lambda is NOT hoisted (only case statements)
case in binding is not hoisted, case in arg position is hoisted
case in nested let body is NOT hoisted
case in let inside lambda is NOT hoisted
case in lambda inside let body is NOT hoisted
case with let+lambda+app is NOT hoisted
case in triple application LHS IS hoisted
case as second argument IS hoisted
case in both arguments - both hoisted
case in second list element IS hoisted
multiple cases in list - all hoisted
case in pair first element IS hoisted
case in pair second element IS hoisted
case in child let binding hoisted into child
case in child let body hoisted into child
case at top level of child let NOT hoisted
cases in both outer and child - each hoisted locally
lambda after app LHS takes us out of top level
case inside case branch is NOT hoisted
case inside case default branch is NOT hoisted
case in arg position inside case branch IS hoisted
case in let body inside applied case default IS hoisted
case in let body inside applied case branch IS hoisted
case application at top level of binding is NOT hoisted
case application in arg position IS hoisted
case application inside immediately-applied lambda IS hoisted
case application in lambda body is NOT hoisted
